concepts.name: Electrochemical Cells â€“ Galvanic and Electrolytic
concepts.explanation: Galvanic (voltaic) cells convert chemical energy of a spontaneous redox reaction into electrical energy. Electrolytic cells use electrical energy to drive non-spontaneous chemical reactions. Both are electrochemical cells.
prerequisites: RD_03 (Redox reactions in terms of electron transfer)

concepts.name: Daniell Cell â€“ Construction and Working
concepts.explanation: The Daniell cell consists of a zinc electrode dipped in ZnSOâ‚„ solution (anode, oxidation) and a copper electrode dipped in CuSOâ‚„ solution (cathode, reduction). The two half-cells are connected by a salt bridge. The cell reaction is Zn(s) + Cuآ²âپ؛(aq) â†’ Znآ²âپ؛(aq) + Cu(s), with Eآ°cell = 1.10 V.
concepts.formula_reaction: Zn(s) + Cuآ²âپ؛(aq) â†’ Znآ²âپ؛(aq) + Cu(s); Eآ°cell = 1.10 V

concepts.name: Electrode Potential and Standard Electrode Potential (Eآ°)
concepts.explanation: Electrode potential is the potential difference developed between an electrode and its electrolyte at equilibrium. Standard electrode potential (Eآ°) is measured when all species involved are at unit concentration (1 M), gases at 1 bar pressure, and temperature at 298 K.

concepts.name: Standard Hydrogen Electrode (SHE)
concepts.explanation: SHE is the reference electrode assigned a potential of exactly 0.00 V at all temperatures. It consists of a Pt electrode coated with platinum black, dipped in 1 M Hâپ؛ solution, with Hâ‚‚ gas at 1 bar bubbled through it. The half-reaction is 2Hâپ؛ + 2eâپ» â†’ Hâ‚‚.
concepts.formula_reaction: 2Hâپ؛(aq) + 2eâپ» â†’ Hâ‚‚(g); Eآ° = 0.00 V

concepts.name: Cell Representation and EMF Calculation
concepts.explanation: A galvanic cell is represented as: Anode | Anode electrolyte || Cathode electrolyte | Cathode. The cell EMF is E_cell = E_cathode - E_anode. For Daniell cell: Zn(s)|Znآ²âپ؛(aq)||Cuآ²âپ؛(aq)|Cu(s), Eآ°cell = Eآ°Cuآ²âپ؛/Cu - Eآ°Znآ²âپ؛/Zn = 0.34 - (-0.76) = 1.10 V.
concepts.formula_reaction: E_cell = E_cathode - E_anode

concepts.name: Nernst Equation for Electrode Potential
concepts.explanation: The Nernst equation gives the electrode potential at non-standard concentrations. For the half-reaction Mâپ؟âپ؛(aq) + neâپ» â†’ M(s), E = Eآ° - (RT/nF) ln(1/[Mâپ؟âپ؛]). At 298 K, E = Eآ° - (0.059/n) log(1/[Mâپ؟âپ؛]).
concepts.formula_reaction: E = Eآ° - (RT/nF) ln(1/[Mâپ؟âپ؛]); At 298 K: E = Eآ° - (0.059/n) log(1/[Mâپ؟âپ؛])

concepts.name: Nernst Equation for Cell Reaction
concepts.explanation: For a general reaction aA + bB â†’ cC + dD, the Nernst equation is E_cell = Eآ°cell - (RT/nF) ln Q, where Q = [C]ل¶œ[D]لµˆ/[A]لµƒ[B]لµ‡. At equilibrium, E_cell = 0 and Q = K, so Eآ°cell = (RT/nF) ln K.
concepts.formula_reaction: E_cell = Eآ°cell - (RT/nF) ln Q; Eآ°cell = (RT/nF) ln K

concepts.name: Relationship between Eآ°cell, خ”rGآ° and Equilibrium Constant K
concepts.explanation: The standard Gibbs energy change is related to Eآ°cell by خ”rGآ° = -nFEآ°cell. Also, خ”rGآ° = -RT ln K. Therefore, Eآ°cell = (RT/nF) ln K. At 298 K, Eآ°cell = (0.059/n) log K.
concepts.formula_reaction: خ”rGآ° = -nFEآ°cell; خ”rGآ° = -RT ln K; Eآ°cell = (0.059/n) log K (at 298 K)

concepts.name: Conductivity (خ؛) and Resistivity (دپ)
concepts.explanation: Resistivity (دپ) is the resistance of a material of unit length and unit cross-sectional area. Conductivity (خ؛) is the reciprocal of resistivity: خ؛ = 1/دپ. SI unit: S mâپ»آ¹. Resistance R = دپآ·l/A = l/(خ؛آ·A).
concepts.formula_reaction: خ؛ = 1/دپ; R = l/(خ؛آ·A)

concepts.name: Cell Constant (G*)
concepts.explanation: Cell constant is the ratio of the distance between electrodes (l) to their cross-sectional area (A): G* = l/A. It has units of mâپ»آ¹ or cmâپ»آ¹. It is determined by measuring the resistance of a solution of known conductivity (e.g., KCl).
concepts.formula_reaction: G* = l/A = Rآ·خ؛

concepts.name: Molar Conductivity (خ›m)
concepts.explanation: Molar conductivity is the conductivity of a solution containing one mole of electrolyte. It is given by خ›m = خ؛/c, where c is the concentration in mol mâپ»آ³ or mol Lâپ»آ¹. Units: S mآ² molâپ»آ¹ or S cmآ² molâپ»آ¹.
concepts.formula_reaction: خ›m = خ؛/c; خ›m(S cmآ² molâپ»آ¹) = (خ؛ أ— 1000) / Molarity

concepts.name: Variation of Conductivity and Molar Conductivity with Concentration
concepts.explanation: Conductivity (خ؛) decreases with dilution (fewer ions per unit volume). Molar conductivity (خ›m) increases with dilution (volume containing one mole of electrolyte increases). For strong electrolytes, خ›m increases slowly and follows خ›m = خ›mآ° - Aâˆڑc. For weak electrolytes, خ›m increases sharply at low concentrations.

concepts.name: Kohlrausch Law of Independent Migration of Ions
concepts.explanation: Kohlrausch law states that at infinite dilution, the limiting molar conductivity of an electrolyte is the sum of the contributions of its individual ions: خ›mآ° = خ½â‚ٹخ»â‚ٹآ° + خ½â‚‹خ»â‚‹آ°, where خ½â‚ٹ and خ½â‚‹ are the number of cations and anions per formula unit, and خ»آ° are their limiting molar conductivities.
concepts.formula_reaction: خ›mآ° = خ½â‚ٹخ»â‚ٹآ° + خ½â‚‹خ»â‚‹آ°

concepts.name: Applications of Kohlrausch Law
concepts.explanation: Kohlrausch law is used to: (i) calculate خ›mآ° for weak electrolytes (cannot be obtained by extrapolation); (ii) determine the degree of dissociation (خ±) for weak electrolytes: خ± = خ›m / خ›mآ°; (iii) calculate the dissociation constant Ka using Ostwald's dilution law: Ka = cآ·خ±آ²/(1-خ±) = cآ·خ›mآ² / [خ›mآ°(خ›mآ° - خ›m)].
concepts.formula_reaction: خ± = خ›m / خ›mآ°; Ka = cآ·خ›mآ² / [خ›mآ°(خ›mآ° - خ›m)]

concepts.name: Faraday's First Law of Electrolysis
concepts.explanation: The amount of chemical reaction (mass deposited or dissolved) at any electrode is directly proportional to the quantity of electricity (charge) passed through the electrolyte: m âˆ‌ Q, or m = Zآ·Iآ·t, where Z is the electrochemical equivalent.
concepts.formula_reaction: m = Zآ·Iآ·t; Z = Molar mass / (nآ·F)

concepts.name: Faraday's Second Law of Electrolysis
concepts.explanation: When the same quantity of electricity is passed through different electrolytes, the amounts of substances liberated are proportional to their chemical equivalent weights (Atomic mass / number of electrons involved in the electrode reaction).

concepts.name: Faraday Constant (F)
concepts.explanation: The Faraday constant (F) is the charge on one mole of electrons. F = N_A أ— e = 96487 C molâپ»آ¹ â‰ˆ 96500 C molâپ»آ¹. One Faraday of charge deposits one gram equivalent of a substance.
concepts.formula_reaction: F = 96487 C molâپ»آ¹ â‰ˆ 96500 C molâپ»آ¹

concepts.name: Products of Electrolysis â€“ Factors Affecting
concepts.explanation: Products of electrolysis depend on: (i) nature of the electrolyte (molten or aqueous); (ii) nature of electrodes (inert or reactive); (iii) standard electrode potentials of possible reactions; (iv) overpotential (extra voltage required due to kinetic factors).

concepts.name: Electrolysis of Aqueous NaCl â€“ A Case Study
concepts.explanation: In aqueous NaCl, at cathode: Hâپ؛ (from water) is reduced to Hâ‚‚ (Eآ° = 0.00 V) rather than Naâپ؛ (Eآ° = -2.71 V). At anode: Clâپ» is oxidized to Clâ‚‚ (Eآ° = 1.36 V) due to overpotential of Oâ‚‚ formation. Products: Hâ‚‚ at cathode, Clâ‚‚ at anode, and NaOH in solution.
concepts.formula_reaction: Cathode: 2Hâ‚‚O + 2eâپ» â†’ Hâ‚‚ + 2OHâپ»; Anode: 2Clâپ» â†’ Clâ‚‚ + 2eâپ»

concepts.name: Primary Batteries â€“ Dry Cell (Leclanchأ© Cell)
concepts.explanation: A dry cell consists of a zinc container (anode) and a carbon (graphite) rod (cathode) surrounded by MnOâ‚‚ and carbon powder. The electrolyte is a paste of NHâ‚„Cl and ZnClâ‚‚. The cell reaction produces about 1.5 V. It is not rechargeable.
concepts.formula_reaction: Anode: Zn â†’ Znآ²âپ؛ + 2eâپ»; Cathode: 2MnOâ‚‚ + 2NHâ‚„âپ؛ + 2eâپ» â†’ 2MnO(OH) + 2NHâ‚ƒ

concepts.name: Primary Batteries â€“ Mercury Cell
concepts.explanation: The mercury cell consists of a zinc-mercury amalgam anode and a HgO + carbon paste cathode, with a KOH and ZnO electrolyte. The cell potential is approximately 1.35 V and remains constant during its lifetime (no ion concentration change). It is used in hearing aids and watches.
concepts.formula_reaction: Anode: Zn(Hg) + 2OHâپ» â†’ ZnO + Hâ‚‚O + 2eâپ»; Cathode: HgO + Hâ‚‚O + 2eâپ» â†’ Hg + 2OHâپ»

concepts.name: Secondary Batteries â€“ Lead Storage Battery
concepts.explanation: A lead storage battery has a lead anode, a PbOâ‚‚ cathode, and 38% Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„ as electrolyte. On discharge: Pb + PbOâ‚‚ + 2Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„ â†’ 2PbSOâ‚„ + 2Hâ‚‚O (Eآ°cell â‰ˆ 2 V). On charging, the reaction is reversed. It is rechargeable and used in automobiles and inverters.
concepts.formula_reaction: Pb + PbOâ‚‚ + 2Hâ‚‚SOâ‚„ â†’ 2PbSOâ‚„ + 2Hâ‚‚O (discharge)

concepts.name: Secondary Batteries â€“ Nickel-Cadmium (Ni-Cd) Cell
concepts.explanation: The Ni-Cd cell has a cadmium anode and Ni(OH)â‚ƒ cathode, with a KOH electrolyte. It is rechargeable, has a longer life than lead storage batteries, but is more expensive. Overall reaction during discharge: Cd + 2Ni(OH)â‚ƒ â†’ CdO + 2Ni(OH)â‚‚ + Hâ‚‚O.
concepts.formula_reaction: Cd + 2Ni(OH)â‚ƒ â†’ CdO + 2Ni(OH)â‚‚ + Hâ‚‚O (discharge)

concepts.name: Fuel Cells â€“ Hydrogen-Oxygen Fuel Cell
concepts.explanation: Fuel cells directly convert the chemical energy of combustion of fuels (Hâ‚‚, CHâ‚„, CHâ‚ƒOH) into electrical energy. In the Hâ‚‚-Oâ‚‚ fuel cell, Hâ‚‚ is fed to the anode and Oâ‚‚ to the cathode, with an electrolyte (KOH). The overall reaction is 2Hâ‚‚ + Oâ‚‚ â†’ 2Hâ‚‚O. Efficiency is about 70% (vs 40% for thermal plants).
concepts.formula_reaction: Anode: 2Hâ‚‚ + 4OHâپ» â†’ 4Hâ‚‚O + 4eâپ»; Cathode: Oâ‚‚ + 2Hâ‚‚O + 4eâپ» â†’ 4OHâپ»; Overall: 2Hâ‚‚ + Oâ‚‚ â†’ 2Hâ‚‚O

concepts.name: Corrosion as an Electrochemical Phenomenon
concepts.explanation: Corrosion (e.g., rusting of iron) is an electrochemical process. At the anodic spot, Fe is oxidised to Feآ²âپ؛ (Fe â†’ Feآ²âپ؛ + 2eâپ»). At the cathodic spot, Oâ‚‚ is reduced in the presence of Hâپ؛ (Oâ‚‚ + 4Hâپ؛ + 4eâپ» â†’ 2Hâ‚‚O). The Feآ²âپ؛ is further oxidised to Feآ³âپ؛, forming hydrated ferric oxide (rust).
concepts.formula_reaction: Anode: 2Fe â†’ 2Feآ²âپ؛ + 4eâپ»; Cathode: Oâ‚‚ + 4Hâپ؛ + 4eâپ» â†’ 2Hâ‚‚O; Overall: 2Fe + Oâ‚‚ + 4Hâپ؛ â†’ 2Feآ²âپ؛ + 2Hâ‚‚O

concepts.name: Prevention of Corrosion
concepts.explanation: Corrosion is prevented by: (i) surface coating (paint, oil, grease); (ii) coating with other metals (galvanisation with Zn, tinning with Sn); (iii) electrochemical protection using a sacrificial electrode (Mg or Zn) that corrodes instead of the protected metal.

concepts.name: Hydrogen Economy
concepts.explanation: The hydrogen economy envisions using hydrogen as a clean, renewable, non-polluting fuel. Hydrogen can be produced by electrolysis of water using solar energy and used in fuel cells to generate electricity, with water as the only by-product.
